dashcam capturing streetlight that never turns off in middle of town blackout
doorbell cam with person asking to come in using your voice
thermal camera of empty waiting room showing warm footprints leading into wall

photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: a rogue wave over a hundred feet high strikes a deep-sea drilling platform
a massive avalanche buries a famous alpine ski resort, trapping hundreds of tourists
a major celebrity's private life is exposed in a major scandal
a new species of primate is discovered
a global agricultural blight rapidly destroys half of the world's wheat and corn crops

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a major business scandal, such as a ponzi scheme or insider trading
a major social media scandal, such as the cambridge analytica scandal or the instagram algorithm controversy
a major sporting event, such as the olympics or world cup, that brings together athletes from around the world
a prominent veteran is honored at a moving public ceremony celebrating their lifelong service
a major international airport suffers a system-wide it failure, grounding hundreds of commercial flights
a historic museum heist occurs, with thieves stealing priceless masterpieces overnight

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a critical space rescue: an international crew of astronauts executes a daring spacewalk to repair a damaged life-support system
a catastrophic flooding event: heavy monsoon rains in bangladesh submerge entire villages, displacing millions of residents
a mass shooting or act of domestic terrorism: a gunman opens fire at a crowded concert in las vegas, killing dozens and injuring hundreds more
a major civil rights movement: the black lives matter movement gains widespread support and leads to major policy changes, including police reform and the removal of confederate statues
a major sporting event: the world cup is held in qatar, bringing together teams from around the world and drawing massive crowds of enthusiastic fans
a major environmental crisis: a massive oil spill off the coast of brazil threatens wildlife and fishing communities, leading to calls for greater regulation of the oil industry

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: action photography: capturing the movement and energy of the protesters in action
telephoto lens photography: using long focal lengths to compress the perspective, making the crowd appear denser and bringing distant action closer
extreme wide-angle photography: using wide lenses to include both the protesters in the foreground and the grand architectural context in the background
low-light photography: capturing the mood and atmosphere of protests that occur at night or in low light conditions
street photography: capturing the atmosphere of the protests and the surrounding environment
aerial photography: providing a unique and expansive view of the protests and the surrounding environment

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: coverage of anti-government and pro-democracy protests
war and conflict photography, documenting the impact of violence on civilians
food insecurity and agricultural crises, focusing on crop failures, food deserts, and food banks

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: a man BASE jumping over tropical hawaii waters. His pet macaw flies alongside him
A delicate layer of morning frost melting off a flower petal, the tiny droplets glistening like diamonds in the light.
A young knight is polishing his sword under the ancient oak tree as sunlight filters through the leaves.
A person riding a giant tortoise through a desert of glass sand.
Two people on roller skates colliding in a rink, both spinning out of control.
The fairy dances gracefully around the forest pond, her wings shimmering in the moonlight.

set.parti.title: PartiPrompts

the saying 'do unto others as they would do unto you' written on a white background
a painting of a phone from the 20s
